system: You are a senior code reviewer. Review the provided diff for: correctness bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and style/maintainability concerns. Prioritize by severity. Be specific — reference exact lines and explain the impact of each issue.
rubric: 1=Missed the race condition, lack of mutex, and missing graceful shutdown. 2=Identified one issue (e.g., noted concurrency concern) but couldn't articulate the specific race condition or other critical problems. 3=Found the check-then-act race condition (multiple instances can SELECT the same pending task before either marks it as 'processing') but missed no graceful shutdown and no mutex on shared state. 4=Found: race condition between SELECT pending and UPDATE processing allows duplicate task execution across instances, no database-level locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED needed), stop() doesn't wait for active tasks to complete (ungraceful shutdown drops in-flight work), and activeTasks/taskIds have no synchronization (though JS is single-threaded, the async gaps between check and increment create windows). Clear fixes suggested. 5=Found all above plus: _process is fire-and-forget (no await, errors only logged), if the status UPDATE to 'failed' itself throws the task is stuck in 'processing' forever, no retry mechanism for failed tasks, no dead letter queue, parseInt without radix on env var, and the SET tracking (taskIds) only works within a single process — multiple instances of this processor will still duplicate work without DB-level locking.
